fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 14-year-old male brought by stepmother after expressing he would 'rather be dead' than continue living in blended family.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 14-year-old male presenting after telling stepmother he would rather be dead than keep living in their blended family. Parents divorced two years ago; father remarried one year ago. Patient reports feeling like an outsider in new family with stepmother and two stepsiblingsEndorses passive thoughts of preferring not to be alive but denies plan, method, or intent. Describes feeling his father has 'moved on' and he no longer matters. Sleep normal, appetite good. Still plays baseball. Has close friends. Sees mother on weekends. No prior mental health treatment. (Social Worker Patricia Henderson, LCSW)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Passive suicidal ideation without plan or intent; feelings of exclusion

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Adjustment disorder with depressed mood related to blended family dynamics; passive suicidal ideation.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Blended family for one year; feelings of exclusion; father remarried; maintains relationship with mother; baseball involvement; close friends.
Assessment_and_Plan: Low risk: passive SI without plan or intent in context of blended family adjustment.
Protective factors: mother relationship, friends, baseball, good insight.
Normal adjustment reaction to feeling displaced in blended family.
Family therapy essential for blended family integration.
Individual therapy for processing divorce and new family dynamics.
One-on-one time with father to maintain connection.
Encourage continued baseball and peer relationships.
Safety plan with stepmother and father.
Follow-up with family therapist within one week.
Safe for discharge with family therapy engagement.
fields.Author: Patricia Henderson, LCSW (Social Worker)
